title: Forward Kinematics
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Forward Kinematics: Forward kinematics: compute end-effector pose from joint angles using DH parameter chain. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.dh.transformation_matrix…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~20; DH_calibration_error, joint_flexibility set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: T_0_n = prod_{i=1}^{n} T_{i-1}^i(q_i); T = Rot * Trans using DH convention
E.world_state_x: end-effector pose (position + orientation) in task space

W.regime: Existence of the recovered cartesian_pose_vector is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 20); DH_calibration_error dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: position accuracy < 1 mm for well-calibrated robot. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~20. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |DH_calibration_error|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
